originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&__x_4` is never used.

sanitizedDescription: The value written to `&x` is never used.
reasoning: Duplicate of TRANSFORMATION_30: same source file (7acf6cd80b2.c), same line (L8, int x = *inode), same PC (CONFIG_DEVPTS_MULTIPLE_INSTANCES), same bug. x is assigned from the potentially-null inode pointer and never read. Marked // ERROR (7) as a VBDB-known bug. This alarm is from a different analysis tool (DEAD_STORE/Pulse vs deadcode.DeadStores/CSA). Correctly constrained, no library macros.
